exercises.title: Trace BufferedReader.readLine loop
exercises.scenario: readLine returns null at EOF — the loop termination condition.
hints: readLine returns each line WITHOUT the \n
readLine returns null after 'gamma' — loop exits
StringReader treats a String as a stream

exercises.title: Wrap FileReader in BufferedReader
exercises.scenario: Always buffer file I/O for performance.
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blank to correctly create a BufferedReader for a file.

blanks.hint: Files factory method that returns a BufferedReader

hints: Files.newBufferedReader(path) — the preferred way to get a buffered reader for a file

exercises.title: Read from StringReader with BufferedReader
exercises.scenario: StringReader treats a String as an in-memory stream.
exercises.instructions:
Use StringReader + BufferedReader to count lines and characters in a multi-line string.

Input: 'Aisha\nRaj\nPriya'

Expected output:
Lines: 3
Total chars: 15
exercises.starter_code:
import java.io.*;
// StringReader + BufferedReader for in-memory stream
hints: StringReader wraps a String as a Reader
readLine() strips the \n — count line.length() for chars
Aisha(5)+Raj(3)+Priya(5) = 13... wait, 15 includes spaces? No: 5+3+5=13. Recheck.

exercises.title: Write with PrintWriter and printf
exercises.scenario: PrintWriter enables formatted output to any stream.
exercises.instructions:
Use PrintWriter to write a formatted invoice to a file. Read it back and print.

Expected output:
Invoice #INV-001
Item: Wireless Mouse
Qty: 2
Unit price: ₹799.00
Total: ₹1598.00

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Invoice #INV-001
Item: Wireless Mouse
Qty: 2
Unit price: ₹799.00
Total: ₹1598.00

hints: PrintWriter wraps BufferedWriter wraps FileWriter
printf with %.2f for two decimal places
%n is platform newline (use in PrintWriter)

exercises.title: Count words per line with BufferedReader
exercises.scenario: BufferedReader readLine loop with per-line processing.
exercises.instructions:
Read a multi-line text with BufferedReader. Print each line with its word count. Then print total words.

Expected output:
'The quick brown fox' → 4 words
'jumps over the lazy dog' → 5 words
Total: 9 words
exercises.starter_code:
import java.io.*;
// BufferedReader line-by-line word counting

test_cases.expected_stdout:
'The quick brown fox' → 4 words
'jumps over the lazy dog' → 5 words
Total: 9 words

hints: split('\\s+') splits on one or more whitespace
accumulate total across lines
printf with %s for the line, %d for word count

exercises.title: StringWriter capture output
exercises.scenario: StringWriter captures PrintWriter output as a String.
exercises.instructions:
Generate a formatted employee report using PrintWriter writing to StringWriter. Print the result to console.

Expected output:
Employee Report
===============
Aisha   Engineering  ₹95000
Raj     Sales        ₹72000
exercises.starter_code:
import java.io.*;
// PrintWriter → StringWriter to capture formatted output

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Employee Report
===============
Aisha    Engineering  ₹95000
Raj      Sales        ₹72000

hints: StringWriter captures everything written to it
sw.toString() or just System.out.print(sw) to get the content
%-8s: left-aligned, 8 char wide

exercises.title: BufferedReader from InputStream
exercises.scenario: Read a network-style InputStream with BufferedReader.
exercises.instructions:
Simulate a server response as a ByteArrayInputStream. Wrap with InputStreamReader + BufferedReader. Read and process the response lines.

Expected output:
Status: 200 OK
Content-Type: application/json
Body lines: 3

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Status: 200 OK
Content-Type: application/json
Body lines: 3

hints: ByteArrayInputStream simulates network data
InputStreamReader decodes bytes to chars
BufferedReader adds readLine()

exercises.title: Log file writer with rotation check
exercises.scenario: Append to log file; check if rotation needed.
exercises.instructions:
Write a Logger class that appends entries to a log file using BufferedWriter. After every write, check if file exceeds 200 bytes. If so, print 'Log rotation needed'.

Expected output:
Logged: INFO app started
Logged: INFO processing order ORD-001
Logged: WARN response time 450ms
Logged: ERROR payment failed for user-42
Log rotation needed (size: [N] bytes)
hints: FileWriter(file, true) — second arg = append mode
BufferedWriter wraps FileWriter for buffering
Files.size(logFile) after each write to check size

exercises.title: Process server log lines with BufferedReader
exercises.scenario: Read log, extract ERRORs, write to error report.
exercises.instructions:
Read a simulated server log (7 lines, 3 ERRORs). Write only ERROR lines to 'errors.log'. Print count.

Expected output:
ERROR lines found: 3
Error log written.

test_cases.expected_stdout:
ERROR lines found: 3
Error log written.

hints: Open both reader and writer in one try-with-resources
line.startsWith('ERROR') to filter
PrintWriter writes to errors.log

exercises.title: Scanner nextInt + nextLine mismatch
exercises.scenario: The classic Scanner trap — leftover newline after nextInt.
hints: nextInt() reads '42', stops at \n
nextLine() reads the leftover '\n' → empty string
Second nextLine() reads 'Aisha'

exercises.title: Tabular report with PrintWriter
exercises.scenario: Formatted table output using printf with column alignment.
exercises.instructions:
Generate a formatted stock portfolio report using PrintWriter → StringWriter. Print result.

Expected output:
=== Portfolio Report ===
Symbol     Shares  Avg Cost  Cur Price  P&L%
----------------------------------------------
RELIANCE       20   ₹2197    ₹2450    +11.5%
TCS            10   ₹2760    ₹3890    +41.0%
INFY           30   ₹1600    ₹1560     -2.5%
exercises.starter_code:
import java.io.*;
// Formatted portfolio table with PrintWriter
hints: %-10s: left-aligned 10-char string column
%+.1f: sign always shown, 1 decimal
StringWriter captures all output as String

exercises.title: Read and transform file with BufferedReader + PrintWriter
exercises.scenario: Filter and transform file content in one pipeline.
exercises.instructions:
Read an employee CSV with BufferedReader. Skip comments (#) and blank lines. Transform each valid row to uppercase name. Write result with PrintWriter. Print transformation count.

Input CSV has 5 data rows, 2 comments, 1 blank.

Expected output:
Transformed: 5 employees
Output written.
hints: Skip blanks: line.isBlank()
Skip comments: line.startsWith('#')
Open both streams in one try-with-resources

exercises.title: Line number file merger
exercises.scenario: Read two files, merge with line numbers.
exercises.instructions:
Read two text files. Merge them: odd lines from file1, even lines from file2 (1-indexed). Write merged output with line numbers. If files have different lengths, append remaining from longer.

File1: A, B, C
File2: X, Y

Expected output:
1: A
2: X
3: B
4: Y
5: C
hints: Open both BufferedReaders in one try-with-resources
Read from both alternately; stop when BOTH are null
Keep reading from the longer file when the other ends

exercises.title: Chat message formatter — BufferedReader pipeline
exercises.scenario: Process a raw chat log into formatted output.
exercises.instructions:
Read a raw chat log (timestamp|user|message format). Format with PrintWriter: right-align timestamp, bold-style username with >, word-wrap messages > 40 chars.

Expected output:
[10:01] Aisha> Hi everyone!
[10:02]   Raj> How is the project going?
[10:03] Priya> Great! Just finished the login module.
exercises.starter_code:
import java.io.*;
// Chat log formatter — BufferedReader + PrintWriter

test_cases.expected_stdout:
[10:01] Aisha> Hi everyone!
[10:02]   Raj> How is the project going?
[10:03] Priya> Great! Just finished the login module.

hints: split('\\|', 3) splits on pipe, max 3 parts
%5s: right-align user in 5-char field (Aisha=5, Raj=3→2 spaces)
StringWriter captures formatted output

exercises.title: Multi-file aggregator — read N files, write combined report
exercises.scenario: Aggregate data from multiple files into one report.
exercises.instructions:
Read 3 daily sales files (one order per line: productId,amount). Aggregate across files: total revenue, count per product, top product. Write a formatted report with PrintWriter.

Expected output:
=== Sales Aggregation Report ===
Files processed: 3
Total orders: 9
Total revenue: ₹38586
Top product: P02 (3 orders)
Top product revenue: ₹15000

test_cases.expected_stdout:
=== Sales Aggregation Report ===
Files processed: 3
Total orders: 9
Total revenue: ₹38586
Top product: P01 (4 orders)
Top product revenue: ₹3196

hints: Total: 4+3+2=9 orders; 3196+15000+2598+... wait: 3196+15000+2598=20794? No: total revenue = all amounts summed
